summary: Noisy audience recording masks a decent 1994 show with solid first‑set highlights like West LA, Peggy‑O and an excellent Rain cover.
review: The tape suffers from high‑level noise and break‑up, making it difficult to raise the volume without distortion. Musically the performance is respectable – the first set delivers strong moments (West LA, Roses, Peggy‑O, Masterpiece) and the second set opens with a standout Beatles cover, Rain, though the overall song selection there was less inspiring for some listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is decent to good while recording quality is poor; weighted average yields an overall three‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Excessive noise and breakup at higher volumes; limited listenability beyond low levels.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid with several strong picks; second set opens strongly but overall song choices are uneven.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Positive remarks on his contributions to West LA, Roses and Peggy‑O.
band_member_comments.Bob: Consistently great playing noted throughout the first set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Special mention of epic Vince singing (keyboard/vocal contribution).
